This is the GCP analogue to the [AWS CloudFormation (EKS)](../aws/deploy-amazon-eks.mdx) template. The same Datagrok services run on every…

Using AWS Marketplace, you can deploy all [Datagrok components](../../develop/under-the-hood/infrastructure.md) and the required AWS infrastructure from scratch with just a few clicks. If you want…

docs.snippet: Use [Docker Compose on a regular host](deploy-regular.md) on a single EC2 instance, configured with [RDS](https://aws.amazon.com/rds/) for the metadata database and [S3](https://aws.amazon.com/s3/) for file storage. :::tip Prefer the CFN templates for production AWS For new AWS stands the [CloudFormation (EKS)](../aws/deploy-amazon-eks.mdx) or…

docs.title: Regular machine
docs.snippet: Datagrok runs as a set of Docker containers on top of a PostgreSQL metadata database and persistent file storage. This page covers manual on-host installs — bare-metal servers, on-prem VMs, single EC2 / GCE instances, or any other host you manage directly with Docker Compose. The same Datagrok services run on every deployment — see…

docs.snippet: Choose the hosting option that best fits your organization's needs. We take care of everything! Defaults below are pinned to the latest stable Datagrok release and are tested together. Override only when you need to pin a specific release or test a candidate. | Service | Image | Version…

docs.snippet: The Datagrok Helm chart is published as an OCI artifact on Docker Hub. candidate_features.name: ADMET Batch Prediction
candidate_features.description: Predicts ADMET properties for an entire molecule column using Chemprop deep-learning models and appends results as new columns with color-coded values. Accessible via the main menu or…

candidate_features.name: ADMET Info Panel
candidate_features.description: Displays ADMET property predictions for the currently selected molecule in a context-pane panel, with a summary pie chart, color-coded values, and sub-categories including toxicity and drug-likeness.

candidate_features.name: Admetica App
candidate_features.description: A standalone application view (browseable under Chem) for interactively exploring ADMET predictions, including a demo mode that showcases the plugin's main functionality.

candidate_features.name: Hit Triage Integration
candidate_features.description: Integrates ADMET predictions into the Datagrok Hit Triage workflow as a scored triage function, enabling ADMET-based compound prioritization.

candidate_features.name: Alation Catalog Browser
candidate_features.description: Embedded Datagrok app that connects to a configured Alation data catalog instance, letting users browse catalogued data sources, tables, and queries and bring them into Datagrok workflows.

candidate_features.name: DataFrame Creation and I/O
candidate_features.description: Demonstrates creating DataFrames from CSV, JSON, objects, columns, and raw bytes, as well as saving, loading, comparing, appending, joining, and linking tables.

candidate_features.name: Column Operations
candidate_features.description: Shows how to add, access, type-convert, sort, and manipulate DataFrame columns, including virtual columns and image columns.

candidate_features.name: Row Filtering and Bitsets
candidate_features.description: Covers row selection, bitset boolean operations (AND/OR/XOR), aggregation, grouping, iterable rows, and collaborative filter-state synchronisation.

candidate_features.name: Metadata and Semantic Types
candidate_features.description: Demonstrates tagging columns and DataFrames with metadata, detecting and assigning semantic types, qualified numbers, value-matchers, and custom category ordering.

candidate_features.name: Grid Cell Rendering
candidate_features.description: Shows how to implement custom cell renderers, HTML cells, sparklines, format formulas, tooltips, hit-testing, and paint callbacks for the main data grid.

candidate_features.name: Grid Column Configuration
candidate_features.description: Covers column width, visibility, ordering, color-coding, styles, data formatting, controlled values, and scroll positioning in the data grid.

candidate_features.name: Built-in Viewers
candidate_features.description: Samples for every built-in Datagrok viewer: scatter plot, bar/box/line/pie chart, histogram, heat map, trellis, network diagram, globe, markup, form, statistics, and more.

candidate_features.name: Viewer Interaction and Customisation
candidate_features.description: Covers dynamic viewer creation, selection/filter/highlight coordination, custom scatter-plot rendering, filter widgets, and viewer event handling.

candidate_features.name: View Management and Navigation
candidate_features.description: Shows how to open, navigate, and manage application views, including docking, ribbons, toolbox pages, layouts, layout permissions, and virtual views.

candidate_features.name: UI Widgets
candidate_features.description: A catalogue of atomic UI controls: buttons, menus, dropdowns, markdown, icons, images, progress indicators, sliders, tabs, and tooltips.

candidate_features.name: Layout Containers
candidate_features.description: Shows the block, box, panel, flexbox, splitter, and widget-tree layout primitives used to arrange content inside a Datagrok view.

candidate_features.name: Dialogs and Input Forms
candidate_features.description: Covers building modal dialogs, wizards, input forms, combo inputs, custom inputs, parameter validation, and master-detail forms using the JS API.

candidate_features.name: Event Handling
candidate_features.description: Shows how to subscribe to and prevent platform events: table, layout, function, and UI events.

candidate_features.name: Function Registry
candidate_features.description: Covers registering, discovering, calling, and caching registered functions; attaching handlers, markup handlers, and auto-renderers; and managing init hooks.

candidate_features.name: Package Scripting
candidate_features.description: Shows how to write multi-language scripts (Python, R, JS) within a package, access file storage from scripts, inspect the scripting context, and handle errors.

candidate_features.name: Server API (dapi)
candidate_features.description: Shows how to use grok.dapi to save/load entities, fetch external URLs, manage user groups, inspect the current user, and persist user settings.

candidate_features.name: Projects and Spaces
candidate_features.description: Shows how to list, open, and navigate projects and spaces via the JS API.

candidate_features.name: File Management
candidate_features.description: Demonstrates reading files, checking file existence, and accessing the platform file store from JS scripts.

candidate_features.name: Database Queries
candidate_features.description: Shows ad-hoc, parameterized, and dynamic SQL queries against Postgres and MSSQL connections, including in-place DB editing and master-detail query patterns.

candidate_features.name: Cheminformatics
candidate_features.description: Samples for the Chem JS API: molecule rendering, substructure search (SMARTS/molfile/ALS), sketcher, R-group decomposition, MCS, diversity search, and scaffold alignment.

candidate_features.name: Machine Learning
candidate_features.description: Covers ML API samples: PCA, clustering, missing-value imputation, predictive model training, random data generation, and statistical correlation.

candidate_features.name: Application Demos
candidate_features.description: Full mini-application examples (stock-broker, bioreactor, general demo) that wire together multiple API surfaces into a cohesive interactive workflow.

candidate_features.name: DataFrame and Column API
candidate_features.description: Core API for creating, accessing, and comparing DataFrames and Columns, including typed accessors, demo-table helpers, and assertion utilities used across the test suite.

candidate_features.name: Function Output Annotations
candidate_features.description: Tests for the output-annotation system that controls how function results join or replace existing DataFrame, Column, or ColumnList outputs.

candidate_features.name: Vector Functions
candidate_features.description: Tests for the JS API's vectorized column-computation mechanism, covering both fully vectorizable and partially non-vectorizable parameter configurations.

candidate_features.name: Package Function Infrastructure
candidate_features.description: Baseline dummy functions, scripts, and a query that verify fundamental package-level registration, async invocation, and default-value handling in the JS API.

candidate_features.name: Semantic Type Detection
candidate_features.description: Tests the registration and invocation of custom semantic-type detectors that classify column content at runtime.

candidate_features.name: Script Engine and Property Parsing
candidate_features.description: Tests for the script execution engine, covering property annotation parsing, formula evaluation, NaN output handling, and package-level property defaults.

candidate_features.name: UI Input Forms and Dialogs
candidate_features.description: Tests for the Datagrok dialog and input-form system, including custom input renderers, multi-field forms, and dialog lifecycle validation.

candidate_features.name: File and Connector API
candidate_features.description: Tests for the dapi.files API and connector framework, covering local file functions, file-connector queries, and web connector integration.

candidate_features.name: Data Query API
candidate_features.description: Tests for building and running database queries via the JS API, including join-based table queries and Elasticsearch integration.

candidate_features.name: Data Transformation and Table Operations
candidate_features.description: Tests for data normalization, sorting, color coding, and the transformation editor — the core non-scripted table manipulation surface.

candidate_features.name: Data Export
candidate_features.description: Tests for exporting tables and views to various file formats through the JS API.

candidate_features.name: Projects and Collaboration
candidate_features.description: Tests for the project browser, sharing, user registration, and inviting collaborators — the multi-user collaboration surface of Datagrok.

candidate_features.name: UI Navigation and Metadata
candidate_features.description: Tests for platform-level UI navigation including the help system, menu visibility controls, entity tags, favorites, and entity collections.

candidate_features.name: Substructure Filter
candidate_features.description: Integration test for the chemical substructure filter, verifying that structure-based column filtering works end-to-end in the platform.

candidate_features.name: Parquet File Import/Export
candidate_features.description: Lets users open, read, and save Datagrok DataFrames as Apache Parquet files, with WASM-based columnar encoding/decoding. Registered as a platform file handler for .parquet files and as a file…

candidate_features.name: Feather File Import/Export
candidate_features.description: Lets users open, read, and save Datagrok DataFrames as Feather (Arrow IPC stream) files. Registered as a platform file handler for .feather files and as a file exporter menu entry.

candidate_features.name: Benchling App
candidate_features.description: Top-level Datagrok application and tree-browser panel that surfaces all Benchling LIMS data through a unified UI entry point under the Chem menu.

candidate_features.name: Sequence Management
candidate_features.description: Read and create amino-acid (AA) and DNA sequences stored in Benchling, returning structured dataframes for downstream analysis in Datagrok.

candidate_features.name: DNA Oligo Management
candidate_features.description: Retrieve and register short DNA oligonucleotide entries in Benchling, enabling oligo inventory and design workflows from within Datagrok.

candidate_features.name: Assay Data Access
candidate_features.description: Fetch and create assay results and assay runs from Benchling, allowing scientists to pull experimental outcome data into Datagrok for visualization and analysis.

candidate_features.name: Molecule Registry
candidate_features.description: Search and register small-molecule entities in the Benchling registry, bridging Benchling compound records with Datagrok chemical analytics.

candidate_features.name: Plate Management
candidate_features.description: Query and create lab plates in Benchling, giving Datagrok users visibility into physical sample containers and enabling plate-based workflows.

candidate_features.name: Mixture Management
candidate_features.description: Retrieve and create mixture (formulation) records from Benchling, supporting compound formulation and sample preparation tracking inside Datagrok.

candidate_features.name: Project Management
candidate_features.description: List Benchling projects accessible to the authenticated user, enabling project-scoped browsing and filtering of LIMS entities within Datagrok.

candidate_features.name: Macromolecule Detection & Notation Refinement
candidate_features.description: Automatically detects macromolecule semantic types (DNA, RNA, peptides) in columns and refines their notation (HELM, FASTA, BILN, separator) using configurable detectors.

candidate_features.name: Sequence Cell Rendering
candidate_features.description: Renders macromolecule sequence columns in the Datagrok grid with notation-specific cell renderers (FASTA, HELM, BILN, separator, custom) and per-column tooltips and header panels.

candidate_features.name: Sequence Difference Rendering
candidate_features.description: Compares two macromolecule sequence columns to build a per-position difference representation and renders it via a dedicated difference cell renderer.

candidate_features.name: Notation Conversion
candidate_features.description: Converts macromolecule columns between supported sequence notations (FASTA, HELM, BILN, separator, custom) via an interactive conversion dialog.

candidate_features.name: Molecule to HELM Conversion
candidate_features.description: Converts small-molecule structures (molfiles / SDF) to HELM notation by matching atoms against a monomer library, enabling peptide enumeration workflows.

candidate_features.name: Atomic Level Structure
candidate_features.description: Expands macromolecule sequences (HELM / FASTA / BILN) into full atomic-level molblocks and displays 2D / 3D molecular structures in the property panel.

candidate_features.name: File Import / Export (FASTA, BAM)
candidate_features.description: Imports FASTA / nucleotide files and BAM alignment files directly into Datagrok tables and exports sequence columns back to FASTA format.

candidate_features.name: Monomer Library Management
candidate_features.description: Provides a full CRUD UI to manage HELM monomer libraries and collections, including browsing, editing monomers, matching molecules to monomers, and standardising library JSON.

candidate_features.name: Split to Monomers
candidate_features.description: Splits a macromolecule sequence column into individual per-position monomer columns, enabling position-level analysis of peptide libraries.

candidate_features.name: Get Region
candidate_features.description: Extracts a named subsequence region (e.g. CDR loop, variable domain) from a macromolecule column into a new column, via an interactive panel, top-menu action, or region input widget.

candidate_features.name: WebLogo & Composition Analysis
candidate_features.description: Visualizes the positional monomer composition of a sequence set as a WebLogo plot, with aggregate and per-row composition panels.

candidate_features.name: Multiple Sequence Alignment
candidate_features.description: Aligns a set of macromolecule sequences using built-in engine or PepSeA (MAFFT-based) alignment algorithms, including support for non-canonical peptides and HELM.

candidate_features.name: Sequence Space
candidate_features.description: Projects macromolecule sequences into a 2D scatter plot via dimensionality reduction of pairwise distances, enabling visual exploration of chemical diversity.

candidate_features.name: Sequence Activity Cliffs
candidate_features.description: Detects pairs of macromolecule sequences that are structurally similar but have a large difference in a measured biological activity, highlighting SAR discontinuities.

candidate_features.name: Sequence Similarity & Diversity Search
candidate_features.description: Interactive viewers to find the most similar sequences to a query and to pick the most diverse subset from a macromolecule dataset.

candidate_features.name: Subsequence Search & Substructure Filter
candidate_features.description: Searches for a motif or subsequence within macromolecule columns and provides a grid filter widget to interactively narrow rows by substructure match.

candidate_features.name: Sequence Identity & Similarity Scoring
candidate_features.description: Computes per-row identity or monomer-fingerprint similarity scores relative to a reference sequence, adding scored result columns to the table.

candidate_features.name: Antibody Numbering & Annotations
candidate_features.description: Assigns standard antibody numbering schemes (IMGT, Kabat, Chothia, AHo) to sequence columns and lets users view and manage CDR / framework region annotations.
